title: Hello RedwoodSDK
author: Alice Redwood
body:
# Hello RedwoodSDK

Welcome to **RedwoodSDK** — the full-stack framework built for Cloudflare Workers.

## Getting Started

RedwoodSDK makes it easy to build server-rendered React applications that run at the edge.

- Fast cold-start times
- Built-in routing
- React Server Components support

Give it a try and see how quickly you can ship!

title: Cloudflare Edge Tips
author: Bob Cloudman
body:
# Cloudflare Edge Tips

Running code at the **edge** means your users get faster responses no matter where they are in the world.

## Top Tips

1. Use **KV** for low-latency reads of configuration and content.
2. Leverage **Durable Objects** for coordinating stateful workloads.
3. Keep your worker bundles small — every byte counts at startup.
4. Cache aggressively with `Cache-Control` headers.

> The edge is not a magic bullet, but it is a very sharp one.

title: React Server Components 101
author: Carol Components
body:
# React Server Components 101

React Server Components (RSC) let you render components **on the server** without sending their JavaScript to the client.

## Why RSC?

- Smaller client bundles
- Direct access to server-side data sources
- Improved Time to First Byte (TTFB)

## How It Works

Server components are rendered once on the server and streamed to the client as a special React payload. Client components hydrate in the browser as usual.

Combined with RedwoodSDK, RSC is a powerful tool for building fast, data-driven apps.
